title: erianthus divaricatus
intro: Erianthus divaricatus, also known as wild rye or goldenrod, is a perennial herb native to eastern North America. The medicinal properties of Erianthus divaricatus lie in its leaves and flowers, which contain flavonoids and terpenes that exhibit anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of this plant include reducing inflammation and soothing skin irritations, as well as supporting respiratory health due to its expectorant properties. Erianthus divaricatus can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or infusion, and it is also used in herbal blends to aid in digestion and relieve menstrual cramps.
